title: Public verification design pass (Versa tokens, PUBLIC_VERIFICATION_UI_RULES)
business_reason: The public certificate verification pages used legacy classes + hardcoded colors (btn-dark, #5f6368) instead of the Versa design. Re-skin them to the design tokens while honoring PUBLIC_VERIFICATION_UI_RULES (simple, calm, exact-code lookup, only whitelisted fields).

design.pages: /verify (lookup) + /verify/certificate/[code] (result) now use .ds-public + Versa tokens + btn-blue (was btn-dark) + a token-colored status banner; no hardcoded colors. Content unchanged — still ONLY status, candidate, olympiad, award, issued-on, code + integrity badge (no PII/scores/OMR/answer-keys/URLs/audit).

param_14.question: Do the public verification screens look on-brand and follow the public rules?
param_14.answer: Yes. The verify lookup and result pages use the Versa design tokens (calm, centered, white card, brand button) and a clear status banner, while continuing to show ONLY whitelisted public fields (status, candidate, olympiad, award, issue date, code, integrity) — never PII, scores, OMR, answer keys, private URLs or audit data, per PUBLIC_VERIFICATION_UI_RULES.
param_14.security_implications: no change to the whitelist — still only public fields; no PII/internal data; integrity badge preserved.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: rate-limit the public lookup (SEC-006 infra); QR deep-link analytics.

verify: live /verify renders ds-public + btn-blue (no legacy badge/btn-dark)
